text_blocks.snippet: Family reunification counselling in Vancouver, BC, is primarily provided through Ministry of Children and Family Development (MCFD) referrals for families facing child protection concerns, as well as specialized private practitioners for high-conflict separation/divorce cases. Key services focus on rebuilding trust, improving communication, and supporting safe, gradual re-connection between children and parents. 211 British Columbia +2

list.snippet: Family Services of Greater Vancouver (FSGV): Offers free, MCFD-referred Family Preservation and Reunification Services. These programs provide in-home and in-office counseling, therapeutic supervised access, and parenting education to help children return to their families or prevent out-of-home placement.

reConnect Families (Vancouver/Surrey): Specializes in reunification for families with child resistance to visitation, estrangement, or high-conflict divorce. They offer a team approach, including Registered Clinical Counsellors, intensive weekends, and parenting coordination.

Bright Star Counselling (Port Moody): Provides a specialized reunification program (run by Clinical Director Sonja Friedland) that uses play-based therapy to help children, youth, and families reconnect.
Touchstone Family Association: Offers family preservation and reunification services for families in crisis, including in-home counseling and parent-teen mediation.
Leanne Toews (Virtual/Specialized): Offers structured, 6-step facilitated virtual and in-person family reunification, including individual sessions and supervised contact.
Moving Forward Family Services: Provides sliding-scale or low-cost counseling options for families in the Lower Mainland. Bright Star Counselling +7
Referral: Many services (especially at FSGV and Touchstone) require a referral from an MCFD social worker.
Focus: Services aim to address child protection concerns, attachment issues, and high-conflict dynamics.
Approach: Intensive in-home support, parenting skills training, and therapeutic supervised access. 211 British Columbia +5
